Container.Name: ComfyUI-Nvidia
Container.Overview: GPU-accelerated ComfyUI node-based image/video generation, using the community-maintained mmartial/comfyui-nvidia-docker image (Kind Robots does not publish its own ComfyUI image). Requires the Unraid "Nvidia-Driver" plugin installed and a compatible NVIDIA GPU passed through. Splits state into a Run path (venv + ComfyUI install, rebuildable) and a Base Directory (models, custom_nodes, input, output — the data actually worth backing up). Checkpoints and other models are large (several GB each); plan appdata/storage accordingly and consider pointing the Base Directory at bulk storage rather than the cache pool.
Container.Requires: Requires the community "Nvidia-Driver" Unraid plugin and a supported GPU. First start installs ComfyUI and a Python venv into the Run path and can take several minutes with no visible progress in the Unraid UI — watch the container log instead of restarting it. Do not expose this container to the public internet; the ComfyUI Manager can install arbitrary custom nodes and has no authentication of its own.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: ComfyUI web interface and API port.

Config.Name: Run Directory
Config.Description: Runtime state: Python venv and the ComfyUI installation itself. Rebuilt automatically on first start; safe to wipe and let the container reinstall if it ever gets into a broken state, but that costs the ~15GB initial install download again.

Config.Name: Base Directory
Config.Description: Models, custom_nodes, input, and output. This is the data that actually matters — back it up. Checkpoints/LoRAs/VAEs are large; consider a bulk-storage share instead of the cache pool if space is tight.

Config.Name: BASE_DIRECTORY
Config.Description: Tells ComfyUI to read models/input/output/custom_nodes from the Base Directory path above rather than under the Run path. Must match the Base Directory container path; leave at the default unless you changed that path's Target.

Config.Name: WANTED_UID
Config.Description: UID the container's internal 'comfy' user runs as. Default 99 matches Unraid's 'nobody' user so files written to the Base/Run directories are owned correctly for the Unraid UI and other containers to read.

Config.Name: WANTED_GID
Config.Description: GID the container's internal 'comfy' user runs as. Default 100 matches Unraid's 'users' group.

Config.Name: SECURITY_LEVEL
Config.Description: ComfyUI Manager permission level (normal/weak/strict). 'normal' allows installing custom nodes/models through the Manager UI; tighten to 'strict' if this instance is reachable by anyone besides you.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
Config.Description: Which GPUs to expose to the container. 'all' exposes every GPU on the host; set a specific GPU UUID to restrict it on multi-GPU systems.
